_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
- **2011** — Climate Change Authority Act 2011 enacted; Act in force as of 2011 (original commencement). *Source: Federal Register of Legislation, legislation.gov.au*
- **1 July 2012** — Climate Change Authority commenced operations. *Source: Climate Change Authority — Wikipedia, December 2025. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Climate_Change_Authority*
- **December 2012** — CCA completed its first statutory review of the Renewable Energy Target scheme. *Source: Special Reviews, Climate Change Authority. https://www.climatechangeauthority.gov.au/special-reviews*
- **February 2014** — CCA completed Review of Australia's greenhouse gas emissions reduction goals. *Source: Special Reviews, Climate Change Authority. https://www.climatechangeauthority.gov.au/special-reviews*
- **22 December 2014** — CCA released statutory review of the Carbon Farming Initiative. *Source: Legislative Reviews, Climate Change Authority. https://www.climatechangeauthority.gov.au/publications/legislative-reviews*
- **14–15 September 2022** — Consolidated version C2022C00255 (C04) registered; Climate Change (Consequential Amendments) Act 2022 came into force, amending the CCA Act to expand the Authority's role to advise the Minister under the new Climate Change Act 2022. *Source: Federal Register of Legislation, C2022C00255. https://www.legislation.gov.au/Details/C2022C00255; Jones Day, October 2022. https://www.jonesday.com/en/insights/2022/10/new-legislation-shaping-australias-emissions-reduction-policy*
- **1 December 2022** — First Annual Progress Report tabled in Parliament. *Source: Legislative Reviews, Climate Change Authority. https://www.climatechangeauthority.gov.au/publications/legislative-reviews*
- **11 December 2017** — CCA released latest (at time) legislative review of the Emissions Reduction Fund. *Source: Legislative Reviews, Climate Change Authority. https://www.climatechangeauthority.gov.au/publications/legislative-reviews*
- **18 December 2023** — CCA published Fourth Review of the CFI Act (ACCU Scheme). *Source: CCA 2023 Review of the ACCU Scheme, DCCEEW. https://www.dcceew.gov.au/climate-change/emissions-reduction/accu-scheme/reviews-and-reforms/cca-review-accu-scheme*
- **2023** — CCA published Fourth Review of NGER Act alongside ACCU review (first time both reviews coincided). *Source: 2023 Reviews of Climate Schemes, Climate Change Authority. https://www.climatechangeauthority.gov.au/2023-reviews-climate-schemes*
- **2024–2025** — CCA delivered 2035 Targets Advice to Government, recommending 62–70% reduction from 2005 levels. *Source: Climate Change Authority Homepage. https://www.climatechangeauthority.gov.au/*
- **2025** — Fourth Annual Progress Report published; found Australia accelerating but insufficient. *Source: Climate Change Authority Homepage, 2025. https://www.climatechangeauthority.gov.au/*
- **2026 (current)** — Fifth Review of the Carbon Credits (Carbon Farming Initiative) Act 2011 (ACCU Scheme) underway; public consultation open. *Source: Climate Change Authority Homepage, 2026. https://www.climatechangeauthority.gov.au/*
- **2026 (forthcoming)** — Fifth NGER Act review expected to coincide with or follow ACCU review cycle completion. *(Conditional: date not confirmed; based on 3-year review cycle.)*
- **At least every 5 years (next cycle ~2029)** — CCA to advise on emissions reduction targets for next NDC. *Source: DLA Piper analysis, March 2023. https://www.dlapiper.com/en/insights/publications/2023/03/australias-climate-change-act-potential-implications-on-the-private-sector*
